Simultaneously, GPT-5.5 closed a meaningful gap in offensive cyber automation benchmarks, eroding what had been Anthropic's perceived advantage in this high-stakes domain.

## Codex Expands to Non-Technical Workflows

OpenAI's Codex update includes 42% faster computer use execution, a responsive browser interface, and integration hooks into Microsoft, Google, and Salesforce suites. The onboarding now explicitly encourages non-coders to use the agent for spreadsheet work, document drafting, research, and task planning. The team chose a "dynamic UI" that routes interface design based on task type, rather than adopting toggle-based design patterns. This appears less about interface polish and more about engineering agents that adapt their own presentation layer depending on what they're automating.

Sam Altman and OpenAI leadership framed this as "for everyone, for any task done with a computer"—a clear statement that computer-use agents are no longer a specialist tool. The underlying capability improvement (20% faster execution, 42% faster computer use) matters less than the signal: OpenAI is productizing agent UX, not just publishing benchmark scores.

## Cyber Parity and the Narrowing Gap

GPT-5.5 now scores 71.4% on the UK AI Security Institute's multi-step cyber-attack simulation benchmarks, approaching Claude Mythos Preview's 68.6%. On specific tasks like the TLO chain, performance is near-equivalent (2/10 vs. 3/10 attempts). Artificial Analysis measured GPT-5.5 Pro achieving new SOTA on frontier-science evals while using roughly 60% lower cost and token consumption than prior versions.

The meaningful shift here is not raw capability but efficiency. Anthropic's prior lead in offensive cyber automation was framed as a unique safety/capability position. That narrative no longer holds. Both providers now credibly execute multi-step cyber workflows, and the economic advantage has flipped toward OpenAI on cost-per-output-token. For enterprises evaluating foundation models for high-value automation—whether that's financial analysis, security research, or structured reasoning—the decision tree has narrowed to reliability, integration depth, and price, not differentiated cyber capability.
items.original_title: [AINews] Agents for Everything Else: Codex for Knowledge Work, Claude for Creative Work

items.headline: ChatGPT Images 2.0 sees strong India adoption, muted global growth
items.body:
OpenAI's new image-generation model, ChatGPT Images 2.0, has generated outsized traction in India since its launch last week, where it became the company's largest user base for the feature. However, global adoption metrics reveal a more restrained picture: while the feature drove an 11% week-over-week increase in app downloads, daily active users and sessions grew only 1%, and web traffic rose 1.6%, according to third-party analytics from Sensor Tower and Similarweb. The disparity signals that while the feature appeals to a specific demographic in high-volume markets, it has not yet catalyzed broad engagement across OpenAI's existing user base.

## India Dominates, Emerging Markets Show Spikes

India accounted for approximately 5 million ChatGPT downloads during the launch week, more than double the roughly 2 million downloads in the U.S. OpenAI reported that Indian users are primarily employing Images 2.0 for personal expression—creating studio-style portraits from everyday photos, social media content, avatars, and fantasy-themed visuals that center themselves. The company attributed some of this strength to improvements in non-Latin text rendering, including Hindi and Bengali support, which addresses a significant gap in existing image tools.

Beyond India, several emerging markets showed sharper adoption curves. Pakistan, Vietnam, and Indonesia experienced app download spikes of up to 79% week-over-week during the rollout period. This concentrated growth in specific geographies suggests that image generation tools are being adopted through different consumption patterns across regions, with emerging markets potentially exhibiting stronger new-user demand than mature ones.

## Market Differentiation Amid Intensifying Competition

The regional variation matters because it highlights how AI image tools are being localized and adopted unevenly—a challenge for enterprises building AI infrastructure globally. OpenAI's investments in multilingual text rendering and "thinking" capabilities that refine outputs suggest the company is deliberately targeting markets where language and cultural expression drive adoption. Google's competing image model also saw early strength in India, underscoring how the nation has become a proving ground for image-generation technology.

For practitioners evaluating enterprise AI deployment, the data suggests that regional customization—language support, cultural context, and use-case framing—can materially influence adoption rates even when the underlying technology is identical. The gap between India's engagement (3.4% growth in daily active users) and global engagement (1%) indicates that feature launches require market-specific strategies rather than uniform rollouts.
items.original_title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
